intro: Gliding joints occur where flat bones slide over one another.
notes: They slide to allow limited movement in many directions.
Crucial for balance on uneven ground.

hotspots.label: Fibula
hotspots.description: The Fibula forms the outer bump of the ankle (Lateral Malleolus). It stabilizes the ankle joint.

hotspots.label: Tibia
hotspots.description: The Tibia forms the inner bump of the ankle (Medial Malleolus). It transfers body weight to the foot.
hotspots.examTip: It connects directly to the Talus bone.

hotspots.label: Tarsals
hotspots.description: The 7 Tarsal bones have flat surfaces that slide (glide) over each other. This allows the foot to adjust to uneven ground.
hotspots.examTip: Gliding joints are found in the Wrist (Carpals) and Ankle (Tarsals).
